{"id":"smallhold-ry18","title":"Extend delivery worker to publish due scheduled posts","description":"## What
Extend the in-process delivery worker tokio task to poll for scheduled posts that are now due: `WHERE scheduled_at IS NOT NULL AND scheduled_at <= now() AND published_at IS NULL`. On trigger: set `published_at = now()` and enqueue to delivery_queue atomically in a single transaction. Worker tick interval must be <= 60 seconds so posts go live within 1 minute of their scheduled time.

## Why
Without this, scheduled posts are stored but never published — the feature only half-works. The delivery worker is the correct place to trigger publication since it already owns the delivery_queue.

## Agent team pattern
- Spawn a Research subagent to: read the existing delivery worker code to understand its tick loop structure, transaction patterns, and how it enqueues to delivery_queue; understand the retry/circuit-breaker logic to ensure publishing a scheduled post follows the same flow as a live post
- Spawn an Implementation subagent to: add a poll branch to the worker tick that queries for due scheduled posts, opens a transaction to set published_at = now() and insert delivery_queue rows for each target follower inbox, commits; ensure worker tick interval is <= 60s; handle the case where no posts are due (no-op)
- Spawn a Test subagent to: write a test that inserts a scheduled post with scheduled_at = now()+2s, starts the worker, waits 3s, then asserts published_at is set and delivery_queue contains the expected rows; test that a post with scheduled_at in the future is NOT triggered early

## Test discipline
- Independent oracle: the test inserts a known post at a controlled time and asserts DB state after the worker runs — this is an independent behavioral oracle (DB state), not the publishing code itself
- Test approach: deterministic time-based integration test with a short scheduled_at offset; assert DB column values directly; assert delivery_queue row count matches expected follower count

## Defensive programming checklist
- No `.unwrap()` in handler paths
- Publication (set published_at + enqueue) must be a single atomic transaction — partial publication leaves the post in an inconsistent state
- After setting published_at, the post must appear in timeline queries and the outbox immediately
- Worker must handle zero due posts gracefully (empty result set is not an error)
- If enqueue fails (e.g. no followers), still set published_at so the post is visible locally","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"mark@wolfssl.com","created_at":"2026-04-23T04:18:56Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-04-23T04:18:56Z","metadata":{},"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"","depends_on_id":"smallhold-4iru","type":"relates-to","created_at":"2026-04-22T21:19:06Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","metadata":"{}"}]}
